title: Formal Juhl statement scaffold
summary: Defines normalized syntax equality and data structures for a finite formal Juhl right-hand side, then packages machine-checked order-one and raw order-two identities.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formal equality
mathematicalContent.body: Two FormalExpr values are declared equal when the limited low-order normalizer returns exactly the same syntax tree.

mathematicalContent.heading: Composition-side placeholder
mathematicalContent.body: A composition I=(I₁,…,Iᵣ) is represented syntactically by nesting formal D-operators around f. This is not identified with the actual canonical GJMS composite.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formal statement
mathematicalContent.body: A statement consists of a finite rational linear combination of formal expressions together with normalized syntax equality to formalP N.

mathematicalContent.heading: Implemented orders
mathematicalContent.body: Orders one and two use a one-term right-hand side equal to the already checked raw P₂ or P₄ expression.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines equality of formal expressions as literal equality after applying the restricted normalizer.

correspondence.leanType: theorem refl (A : FormalExpr) : FormalEq A A
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves reflexivity of normalized formal equality.

correspondence.leanType: theorem trans {A B C : FormalExpr} (hAB : FormalEq A B) (hBC : FormalEq B C) : FormalEq A C
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves transitivity of normalized formal equality.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: A\equiv B\land B\equiv C\Longrightarrow A\equiv C.

correspondence.leanType: theorem symm {A B : FormalExpr} (hAB : FormalEq A B) : FormalEq B A
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves symmetry of normalized formal equality.

correspondence.leanType: def formalGJMSCompositionExpr {N : ℕ} (I : Composition N) : FormalExpr
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Represents a composition by nesting formal D-operators according to its ordered parts.
Stores one rational coefficient and one formal expression in a Juhl right-hand side.
Converts one formal term to the scaled expression cE.
Stores a finite ordered list of formal Juhl terms.
Converts a formal right-hand side to the sum of its scaled terms.
Packages a formal order-N right-hand side and a normalized syntax equality with formalP N.
Builds the order-one formal statement with the one-term raw P₂ expression.
Builds the order-two formal statement with the one-term raw P₄ expression.

references.locator: Formal Juhl-type formula and OR bridge
references.note: Primary source for the intended curved OR–Juhl identity.
Source for the Juhl side of the formal syntax.

limitations: The equality is only equality after a weak syntax normalizer. The composition expression uses formal Dⱼ nodes and is explicitly not identified with the ambient GJMS compositions. The order-one and order-two statements are one-term restatements of prior raw checks, not the general Juhl composition formula.
